mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes SLK 55 AMG engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M113 V8 typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only, with the M152 V8 higher at £6,000–£9,500. A partial M113 rebuild starts from around £8,800. Fitting adds £1,300–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your SLK.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes SLK 55 AMG engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends which generation your SLK is. The R171's M113 (2005–2011) is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most robust engines — a 3‑valve SOHC V8 that can exceed 200,000 miles. The R172's M152 (2012–2015) is more advanced but has camshaft adjuster and harmonic damper watchpoints. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the SLK 55 AMG engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: M113 (R171): crankshaft position sensor failure (hot‑start issues), oil leaks (rear main seal, rocker covers), oil pump pickup O‑ring degradation. M152 (R172): camshaft adjuster failure (ticking, oil in wiring loom), harmonic damper degradation, oil leaks from magnesium breather covers.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an SLK 55 AMG engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. An R171 SLK 55 AMG is a £8,000–£18,000 vehicle — a £5,000–£6,000 recon is 28–75% of the car's value. An R172 SLK 55 AMG is a £12,000–£25,000 vehicle — a £6,000–£7,500 recon is 24–63% of the car's value. Evaluate carefully based on your car's generation and condition.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes SLK 55 AMG engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SLK 55 AMG covers M113 (R171, 2005–2011) and M152 (R172, 2012–2015). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm which one you have for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on an SLK 55 AMG?

acceptedAnswer.text: On M113: replace the crankshaft position sensor preventatively at 80,000–100,000 miles. Address oil leaks promptly. On M152: have the camshaft adjusters inspected at 40,000–60,000 miles — catching a failing adjuster early can prevent expensive wiring and engine damage. Both engines benefit from regular oil changes with Mercedes‑approved oil and shortened intervals (5,000–7,500 miles). At the first sign of hot‑start issues, ticking or oil leaks, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes SLK 55 AMG Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the range across both generations; specific costs vary by era — see the Era Map above. A partial M113 rebuild starts from around £8,800. An M152 failure can be very expensive.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an SLK 55 AMG engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). V8 engines require significant disassembly.

cta.label: → Compare SLK 55 AMG engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Crankshaft Position Sensor Failure (M113 — R171, 2005–2011)
problems.symptoms: Engine starts fine when cold but fails to restart when hot; intermittent stalling; engine warning light.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection if hot‑start issues occur

problems.recommendation: The crankshaft position sensor is a common failure point on the M113. Symptoms include starting fine when cold but failing to restart when hot. Mercedes specialist advice is to carry a spare in the glove box. At the first sign of hot‑start issues, have the sensor replaced — it's an inexpensive part that can leave you stranded.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Rear Main Seal & Rocker Covers (M113 — R171, 2005–2011)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, oil residue on the engine, drop in oil level.
problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are common on the M113 as it ages. Rear main seal and rocker cover gaskets are the most frequent sources. If you notice oil spots or a burning smell, have the engine inspected. Replacing gaskets promptly prevents more serious damage and expensive repairs.

problems.title: Camshaft Adjuster Failure (M152 — R172, 2012–2015)
problems.symptoms: Ticking noise from the engine, loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, oil wicking into the wiring loom.
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£4,000 (camshaft adjuster replacement and wiring repair)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £6,000–£9,500 (full engine replacement if damage is extensive)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear ticking or see warning lights, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The M152's camshaft adjusters are a known weakness. They can fail, causing ticking and oil wicking into the wiring loom. If left unaddressed, this can lead to more serious engine damage. At the first sign of ticking or oil in the wiring, have the camshaft adjusters inspected immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: SLK 55 AMG — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — before wiring and engine damage occurs
Little left to salvage — replacement is the only option

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SLK 55 AMG? Check These First
buyIf: * R171 M113: no hot‑start issues — crankshaft sensor in good condition
* R171 M113: no oil leaks — rear main seal and rocker covers dry
* R172 M152: no ticking from the engine — camshaft adjusters in good condition
* Full Mercedes‑AMG service history with regular oil changes
avoidIf: * R171: hot‑start failure with no sensor history — crankshaft sensor may be failing
* R171: oil spots under the vehicle — leaks may be significant
* R172: ticking from the engine — camshaft adjusters may be failing
* R172: oil in the wiring loom — camshaft adjuster failure has occurred
* Poor service history — AMG V8s are sensitive to neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The SLK 55 AMG is a compact V8 roadster with genuine AMG character — a 5.4‑ or 5.5‑litre V8 in a small, lightweight package. The R171's M113 is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most robust engines, with crankshaft sensor and oil leaks as the main watchpoints. The R172's M152 is more advanced, with direct injection and cylinder deactivation, but has camshaft adjuster and harmonic damper watchpoints. Buy on full service history, check for hot‑start issues (M113) and ticking from the engine (M152), and never ignore an oil leak. A well‑maintained SLK 55 AMG is a future classic; a neglected one is a £7,000+ bill waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare SLK 55 AMG engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes SLK 55 AMG (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; M113 should fire immediately, M152 should not tick
* Hot Start — Check if the engine restarts when hot; crankshaft sensor indicator (M113)
* Oil Leaks — Check under the car and around the engine; rear main seal and rocker covers (M113)
* Tick / Rattle — Listen for ticking from the engine; camshaft adjuster indicator (M152)
* Service History — including which generation (R171 or R172) and confirmed oil‑change intervals
* Wiring Loom (M152) — Check for oil in the wiring loom; camshaft adjuster failure indicator
* Harmonic Damper (M152) — Check for play or looseness; harmonic damper degradation indicator
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
